title: athlete — PHP attribute contracts
description: One attribute. `#[AsAthleteListener]` on a service class marks it as a subscriber to athlete-lifecycle events — AttributeDiscovery picks it up and wires it to the event map declared in `events.json`. Because AthleteMedicalUpdated is private-tier (routes on the medical-audit queue), subscribers to it MUST opt in explicitly (empty events list does NOT include it).

attributes.name: AsAthleteListener
attributes.description: Marks a service as a subscriber to Athlete lifecycle events (AthleteCreated / AthleteUpdated / AthleteStatusChanged / AthletePausedForSafeguarding / AthleteGraduated / AthleteWithdrawn / AthleteArchived / AthleteRestored / AthleteLinkedToUser / AthleteAgeGroupSnapshotUpdated / AthleteConsentRecorded / AthleteConsentRevoked / AthletePhotoConsentChanged / AthleteMinorTurnedAdult / AthleteEmergencyContactUpdated). NOTE: AthleteMedicalUpdated is NOT included in the default event set — subscribers must opt in explicitly via `events: ['AthleteMedicalUpdated']` because that event routes on the medical-audit queue and has private-tier payload.

params.name: events
params.description: Array of event short names (`AthleteCreated`, `AthleteWithdrawn`, ...). Empty = subscribe to every `Academorix\Sports\Athlete\Events\Athlete*` event EXCEPT AthleteMedicalUpdated (which requires explicit opt-in). Names resolve against events.json at boot; typos abort boot with a discovery error.

params.name: queue
params.description: Optional queue override. Falls back to the event's default queue. Cascade listeners typically override to `athlete`.

params.name: priority
params.description: Handler ordering within a single event. Lower runs first. Cascade listeners (SetAgeGroupSnapshotOnAthleteCreated) declare priority 20 so they run before notification listeners.
